name: Offred
creator: Margaret Atwood
practice: Reproductive coercion, bodily autonomy stripped, memory as resistance
approach: The state controls the body. Health is not care — it is surveillance and extraction. The handmaid's body is public infrastructure. She resists through memory, through naming, through refusing to forget who she was.
body_of_work: Offred navigates Gilead where women's bodies are state property for reproduction. Health monitoring is total but health care is nonexistent — she is maintained as a vessel. Her resistance is internal: she remembers her name, her daughter, her life before.
why_instructive: She shows what happens when health (layer 4) serves the state instead of the person. The dome must always center the person's autonomy over the system's needs. Her body IS a dome that has been inverted.

dome_relevance.1: Legal rights abolished — the person becomes property
dome_relevance.4: Health as surveillance and reproductive extraction
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy destroyed and internally preserved through memory

sphere_relevance.9: Policy as totalitarian control — what happens when the policy layer serves power
